## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking; fixes an existing issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking; adds new capability)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that changes existing behaviour)
- [ ] Refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Chore (dependency update, CI, build scripts)

## Domain

- [ ] Core OS
- [ ] AI / AURA
- [ ] Bridge (iOS / Android / Linux)
- [ ] Memory
- [ ] Build / Test
- [ ] Docs

## Testing

<!-- Describe the tests you ran and how to reproduce them -->

- [ ] Unit tests pass: `AIOS_HOME=$(pwd) OS_ROOT=$(pwd)/OS bash tests/unit-tests.sh`
- [ ] Integration tests pass: `bash tests/integration-tests.sh`
- [ ] Manually tested on: <!-- Termux | Debian/Ubuntu | macOS | Raspberry Pi -->

## Checklist

- [ ] My branch is based on `dev` (not `main`)
- [ ] Code follows the style of the surrounding files
- [ ] New/changed behaviour is covered by tests
- [ ] Documentation is updated (if applicable)
- [ ] `docs/CHANGELOG.md` is updated under `## [Unreleased]`
- [ ] No breaking changes (or breaking changes are documented and follow the [Deprecation Policy](docs/GOVERNANCE.md#5-deprecation-policy))
- [ ] An RFC exists for this change (if required — see [Governance](docs/GOVERNANCE.md#2-decision-making-rules))
